preview_text:
France enters as a clear favorite with an 83% win probability according to the model, leaving Iraq with only a 1% chance of victory. The statistical forecast positions this as a heavily asymmetric matchup, though Iraq's underdog status does not render the outcome inevitable.

The model assigns a 16% probability to a draw, suggesting the match carries meaningful variance despite France's dominance in the win column. A stalemate remains plausible; France's victory margin reflects quality separation rather than a scenario where only one outcome deserves consideration. Competitive resistance from Iraq is modeled as unlikely but not excluded.

The sportsbook consensus departs noticeably from the model. Books price France at 88%, a 5-percentage-point lift, while trimming the draw to 9% and elevating Iraq to 3%. Polymarket prices follow a similar direction, pushing France toward 90% and compressing the draw further. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap suggests market participants view Iraq's winning chances as meaningfully higher than the model's assessment.
